title: OPEC+ crude exports rise in January while non-OPEC+ dips
section_path: OPEC+ crude exports rise in January while non-OPEC+ dips
section_path_text: OPEC+ crude exports rise in January while non-OPEC+ dips
summary:
Main
Global crude/condensate exports rose m-o-m in January as increases from OPEC+ more than offsetting those from nations outside the producer group. Within OPEC+, Saudi Arabia, Kazakhstan and Iran posted the largest increase in exports, the latter being a potentially a short-lived boost. By Jay Maroo
Global crude/condensate exports started 2025 continuing the trend seen at the end of last year and ended January at 40.6mbd, the highest monthly level since May 2024.
